use std::thread;

fn fetch_data(url: &str) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let rt = tokio::runtime::Runtime::new()?;
    let response = rt.block_on(async {
        let resp = reqwest::get(url).await?;
        let body = resp.text().await?;
        Ok::<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>>(body)
    })?;
    Ok(response)
}

fn background_work(handle: tokio::runtime::Handle) {
    thread::spawn(move || {
        handle.block_on(async {
            let data = fetch_remote_data().await.unwrap();
            process_data(&data);
        });
    });
}

async fn fetch_remote_data() -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> { Ok("data".into()) }
fn process_data(_data: &str) {}
